Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the eyewear catalog, such as eyeglasses, sunglasses, or contact lenses.

category.description: Category of the product (e.g., eyeglasses, sunglasses, contact lenses).

sku.description: Stock keeping unit (SKU) of the product.

LensConfiguration.title: LensConfiguration
LensConfiguration.description: Represents the configuration of lenses for a specific product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lens configuration.

lensType.description: Type of lens (e.g., single vision, progressive).

addOns.description: List of add-ons selected for the lens.

PrescriptionValidation.title: PrescriptionValidation
PrescriptionValidation.description: Represents the validation rules for prescription values.

id.description: Unique identifier for the prescription validation rule.

parameter.description: The prescription parameter being validated (e.g., sphere, cylinder, axis).

minValue.description: Minimum acceptable value for the parameter.

maxValue.description: Maximum acceptable value for the parameter.

userId.description: Reference to User. (Relationship: User 1:N Order). The user who placed the order.

status.description: Status of the order (e.g., pending, processing, shipped, delivered).

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents an item within an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the order item.

quantity.description: Quantity of the product in the order item.

price.description: Price of the product at the time of the order.

ReturnRequest.title: ReturnRequest
ReturnRequest.description: Represents a request to return a product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the return request.

status.description: Status of the return request (e.g., pending, approved, rejected).

requestDate.description: Date the return request was submitted.

Refund.title: Refund
Refund.description: Represents a refund issued to a customer.

paymentMethod.description: Payment method used for the refund.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application. Note: Authentication details (e.g., passwords) should be managed by Supabase Auth and are NOT included here.

definition.description: Stores product information. Admin can read and write. All users can read.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores lens configurations for a product. Admin can read and write. All users can read.

params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

params.name: lensConfigurationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lens configuration.

definition.description: Stores prescription validation rules. Admin only access.

params.name: prescriptionValidationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the prescription validation rule.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Only the user and admin can read and write.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores user orders. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Only the user and admin can read and write.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Stores order items for an order. Only the user and admin can read and write.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item.

definition.description: Stores return requests for a user. Includes denormalized 'orderItemId' for authorization independence. Only the user and admin can read and write.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: returnRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the return request.

definition.description: Stores refund information. Admin only access.

params.name: refundId
params.description: The unique identifier of the refund.

definition.description: Indicates admin privileges for a user. Existence of the document grants admin rights.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage product catalogs, lens configurations, orders, and returns for the Zeno Pure Vision application, focusing on secure and scalable data access. The design emphasizes Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) to avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies, enables secure list operations (QAPs), and maintains data clarity and integrity.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

*   **Orders:** The `orders` collection stores `userId` within each document. This denormalization allows security rules to validate the user's ownership of the order without needing to perform a `get()` on a parent `users` document. This ensures atomic operations.
*   **ReturnRequests:** The `returnRequests` collection stores `orderItemId`, removing the requirement to `get()` parent `orderItems` documents.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**

*   **Products:** Stored in a top-level `products` collection. This is appropriate as all products likely have similar access control requirements (e.g., read access for all users, write access for admins).
*   **PrescriptionValidations:** Stored in a top-level `prescriptionValidations` collection. Access is likely limited to admins, ensuring a homogeneous security posture.
*   **Users:** The `users` collection uses path-based ownership, ensuring that only the user and admins can access user profiles.

**Access Modeling (Standardization and Consistency):**

*   **Private Data:** User-specific data like orders and return requests are nested under the `/users/{userId}` path, representing a `User 1:N Entity` relationship, with ownership being derived from the path itself. This eliminates the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

The structure supports secure `list` operations by:

*   Segregating data with different access needs into separate collections (e.g., `products` which is publicly readable vs `users/{userId}/orders` which is user-specific).
*   Using path-based ownership for user-specific data, allowing rules to efficiently filter data based on `request.auth.uid` without `get()` calls.

**Admin Roles:**

The design assumes the existence of a `roles_admin` collection where documents with a UID indicate administrative privileges. This aligns with the DBAC (Database Authentication Control) principle and promotes simple security rules based on document existence rather than content.

This structure facilitates simple and robust security rules. For example, reading an order requires only checking if the `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` stored in the order document. Creating a return request requires validating that the `orderItemId` corresponds to an order owned by the user. These checks are efficient and atomic, enabling a secure and scalable application.
